texts.full_text: elements, her employer “is vicariously liableper se.” 24 In Burlington Industries, Inc., v. Ellerth,the Supreme Court defined a tangible employment action as change in employment status, such as hiring, firing, failing to promote, reassignment with significantly different responsibilities, or adecision causinga significant change in benefits.” 25TheCourt explained that, in “mostcases,” a tangible employment action “inflicts economic harm.” 26With that principle as its foundation,the Court found thatthe “denial of a raise”was atangible employment action. 27 InRussell v.
